text:
Admission Date: 1985-05-25        Discharge Date: 2018-01-01

Date of Birth:  2015-10-27        Sex:  M

Service:  CSU


HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:  The patient was transferred from
Institute For Orthopedic Surgery Hospital.  The patient is a 47 year old male
with a history of type 1 diabetes mellitus and hyperlipidemia
who presented to the outside hospital at 8:00 p.m. the night
prior to transfer after a soccer match when he developed a
six out of ten pain without any association with exertion,
radiation around the chest and persistent throughout the
night.  At the outside hospital, the patient was afebrile
with electrocardiogram showing that the patient was sinus in
the 90s with J point elevation in V4 and V5 and one negative
set of cardiac enzymes.  The patient's pain, however,
improved with sublingual Nitroglycerin but the patient states
the pain recurred and the patient received Morphine for
relief.  The patient was on Aspirin, Lipitor, Metoprolol,
Lovenox and sublingual Nitroglycerin.  The patient had
another electrocardiogram that showed a 3.[**Street Address(2) 2811**]
depression, and the patient was transferred to San Antonio State Hospital for cardiac catheterization.  The
patient did not have a prior history of chest pain or
pressure, and the patient denied decrease in exercise
tolerance.  The patient denies fever or chills, no nausea,
vomiting or diarrhea.  No metallic taste in the mouth.

ALLERGIES:  No known drug allergies.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  Type I diabetes mellitus, hemoglobin
A1C is 9.0.

Hyperlipidemia.

Thalassemia minor.

SOCIAL HISTORY:  The patient had a ten pack year history and
has quit smoking.  He denies alcohol use or drug use.

FAMILY HISTORY:  No cardiac history in the family.


MEDICATIONS ON ADMISSION:
1. Aspirin.
2. Metoprolol 25 mg p.o. twice a day.
3. Lovenox.
4. Nitroglycerin Paste.
5. Zocor.
6. Colace.


PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:  On physical examination, the patient's
temperature was 98.2, heart rate 68, respiratory rate 20,
blood pressure 110/60, oxygen saturation 99 percent on two
liters.  The patient was alert and oriented in no apparent
distress.  The pupils are equal, round and reactive to light
and accommodation.  Extraocular movements are intact.  The
patient had no jugular venous distention.  No bruits were
noted.  Thyroid was normal.  Respiratory was clear to
auscultation bilaterally.  The patient's heart was regular
with normal S1 and S2 without any murmurs.  The patient had
two plus pulses and carotids, dorsalis pedis and posterior
tibial.  The patient's abdomen was soft, nontender,
nondistended.  Rectal examination revealed guaiac negative.

LABORATORY DATA:  Electrocardiogram showed a regular rate in
the 60s, normal sinus rhythm and normal axis and possible
left ventricular hypertrophy.  On admission, the white blood
cell count was 7.4, hematocrit 33.0, platelet count 153,000.
Sodium 138, potassium 3.8, chloride 100, bicarbonate 20,
blood urea nitrogen 20, creatinine 1.0, glucose 77.

HOSPITAL COURSE:  The patient was admitted to the cardiology
service to undergo an emergent cardiac catheterization.  The
patient's cardiac catheterization showed that the patient had
an ejection fraction of 30 percent with three vessel disease.

The patient underwent coronary artery bypass graft times
four, left internal mammary artery to left anterior
descending coronary artery, saphenous vein graft to diagonal,
saphenous vein graft to obtuse marginal and saphenous vein
graft to right coronary artery.  Please see the operative
note for details.  Postoperatively, the patient was
transferred to Cardiac Surgery Recovery Unit.  On
postoperative day number one, the patient was on Neo-
Synephrine drip.  Heart rate was 95 and sinus.  Blood
pressure was 110/50s with cardiac index of 4.4.  The patient
was net positive approximately one liter.  The patient was
NPO overnight.  Hematocrit was 27.2.  Temperature maximum was
100.9 and white blood cell count was 7.1.  Creatinine was
0.8.  The patient was on preoperative Vancomycin.  The
patient's Neo-Synephrine was weaned, however, the patient
continued to require some support.  The patient's Swan was
removed.  The patient was advanced to cardiac diet and stayed
in the Intensive Care Unit.  On postoperative day number two,
the patient's chest tubes were removed overnight.  The
patient continued to have requirement of Neo-Synephrine at
0.4.  Heart rate was 89 and sinus.  Blood pressure was
101/47.  The patient was net negative approximately one
liter.  He was taking good p.o. and had hematocrit of 23.7
and creatinine of 0.8.  The patient was continued on
Vancomycin and was continued to be weaned on Neo-Synephrine
and was started on iron and Vitamin C and Lasix was held.  On
postoperative day number three, he has had no events.  The
patient was off Neo-Synephrine after receiving a fluid bolus.
The patient was heart rate 86 and sinus, blood pressure of
115/60, was net positive 1.6 liters and was tolerating p.o.
without any difficulties.  Temperature maximum was 100.5 with
a white blood cell count of 5.3 and hematocrit of 23.2 and
creatinine 0.8.  The patient was started on Metoprolol and
Lasix and was transferred to the floor.  The patient's major
issue was blood sugar control.  Consultation with Alvarez was
obtained and recommended starting the home dose of Glargine
and Humalog insulin scale.  On postoperative day number four,
the patient remained afebrile with stable vital signs, with
good urine output with a hematocrit of 24.2 and creatinine of
0.9.  The patient was p.o. Lasix.  The patient's beat wires
were removed and the patient was discharged home after being
cleared by physical therapy.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:  Status post acute coronary syndrome.

Status post coronary artery bypass graft times four.

Type 1 diabetes mellitus.

Hyperlipidemia.

Thalassemia minor.

Remote tobacco history.

MEDICATIONS ON DISCHARGE:
1. Lasix 20 mg p.o. twice a day for five days.
2. K-Dur 20 mEq p.o. twice a day for five days.
3. Colace 100 mg p.o. twice a day.
4. Ascorbic Acid 500 mg p.o. once daily.
5. Lipitor 20 mg p.o. once daily.
6. Plavix 75 mg p.o. once daily for three months.
7. Percocet one to two tablets p.o. q4-6hours p.r.n. pain.
8. Protonix 40 mg p.o. once daily.
9. Iron 150 mg p.o. once daily.
10.      Tylenol and Motrin p.r.n. pain.
11.      Metoprolol 25 mg p.o. twice a day.
12.      Aspirin.


FOLLOW UP:  The patient is to follow-up with Dr. Hernandez in
two weeks.  Please follow-up with Dr. Williams in two weeks.
Please follow-up with Dr. Phillips in one week and please have
blood sugar checked frequently and monitored closely.  Please
follow-up with Dr. Williams in four weeks.

DISPOSITION:  Home with VNA.

CONDITION ON DISCHARGE:  Good.





                        [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Smith, 74820
